problem_statement: Gateway tenant identity can consume channel and authority headers, but trusted identity headers need an explicit boundary judgment that blocks spoofed client-supplied headers unless a verified gateway strips, verifies, injects, and records evidence.
success_criteria: Trusted identity headers are disabled by default

Complete OIDC gateway evidence accepts trusted headers only after client-header stripping, verified injection, issuer pinning, audience binding, fresh JWKS evidence, bypass protection, and retained evidence refs
Complete mTLS gateway evidence accepts trusted headers only after client-header stripping, verified injection, certificate-chain verification, bypass protection, and retained evidence refs
Missing gateway evidence returns explicit blocker codes
Malformed evidence refs and non-boolean evidence fields raise explicit errors
The assessment does not authenticate requests, create tenant mappings, mutate stores, or grant production authority
non_goals: no deployment or production readiness claim
constraints: trusted headers must not be accepted from clients directly
identity header evidence must be gateway-bound and receipt-backed
OIDC and mTLS verifier paths must remain explicit
errors must be deterministic and bounded
evidence_required: focused tenant identity boundary test receipt
standalone SDLC requirement and design validation receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
